title: Control Effectiveness Trending

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.5.36 demands compliance with information security policies, rules and standards; measuring control pass/fail rates over time gives the organisation the operational evidence Clause 9.1 (monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation) requires.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO 27001 A.5.36 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.9.3: Control Effectiveness Trending.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.9.3' running on index compliance and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: ID.IM-02 asks that improvements are identified from evaluations of cybersecurity capabilities. Effectiveness trending by control domain is the direct input signal for that improvement cycle.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST CSF ID.IM-02 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.9.3: Control Effectiveness Trending.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.9.3' running on index compliance and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Tenable (Splunkbase 4060) for scan-based control checks, DB Connect for ITGC spreadsheets, or `compliance:control_test` automation feeds
spl:
index=compliance sourcetype IN ("compliance:control_test","nessus:sc:compliance","qualys:policy") earliest=-90d
| eval outcome=if(match(coalesce(test_result,status,result),"(?i)pass|passed|success|green"),1,0)
| eval domain=coalesce(control_domain,pluginFamily,"UNCLASSIFIED")
| bin _time span=7d
| stats count as tests, sum(outcome) as passes by _time, domain
| eval pass_ratio=if(tests>0, round(100*passes/tests, 2), null())
| sort domain, _time
| streamstats window=4 avg(pass_ratio) as eff_trend by domain
| table _time, domain, tests, pass_ratio, eff_trend
```
```spl
index=compliance sourcetype IN ("compliance:control_test","nessus:sc:compliance") earliest=-90d
| eval outcome=if(match(coalesce(test_result,status),"(?i)pass|passed|success"),1,0)
| bin _time span=7d
| stats count as tests, sum(outcome) as passes by _time
| eval org_pass_ratio=if(tests>0, round(100*passes/tests,2), null())
| sort _time
| trendline sma3(org_pass_ratio) as ratio_trend
| eventstats avg(org_pass_ratio) as portfolio_mean
| eval gap=round(org_pass_ratio-portfolio_mean,2)
| predict org_pass_ratio as ratio_forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=2 period=6
description: A ninety-day pass ratio by control domain exposes domains where tests are failing more often or trending down—so you prioritize control owners, evidence collection, and automation before a failed external assessment.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001, NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes compliance and sourcetypes (, compliance:control_test, nessus:sc:compliance. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Map vendor fields (`pluginFamily`, Qualys title) to internal `control_domain` via lookup `control_domain_map.csv`; (2) dedupe repeated tests per asset/control daily to avoid skew; (3) review domains where `eff_*` slopes negative for four consecutive buckets; (4) tune `span=7d` to match test frequency; (5) store weekly CSV exports for assessors. Indexes required: `index=compliance`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=(`, `sourcetype=compliance:control_test`, `sourcetype=nessus:sc:compliance`, `sourcetype=qualys:policy`.
visualization: Multiseries line or area (pass_ratio by domain), heatmap (domain x week), line (`org_pass_ratio`, `ratio_trend`, `ratio_forecast`).
cimModels: Vulnerabilities (when Tenable/Qualys maps to CIM); otherwise N/A
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t sum(Vulnerabilities.cvss) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest span=7d | sort - agg_value
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We monitor a ninety-day pass ratio by control domain exposes domains where tests are failing more often or trending down—so you prioritize control owners, evidence collection, and automation before a failed e. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Tenable (Splunkbase 4060) for scan-based control checks, DB Connect for ITGC spreadsheets, or `compliance:control_test` automation feeds.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=compliance` `sourcetype IN ("compliance:control_test","nessus:sc:compliance","qualys:policy")` — `control_domain`, `test_result` or `status`, `_time`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map vendor fields (`pluginFamily`, Qualys title) to internal `control_domain` via lookup `control_domain_map.csv`; (2) dedupe repeated tests per asset/control daily to avoid skew; (3) review domains where `eff_*` slopes negative for four consecutive buckets; (4) tune `span=7d` to match test frequency; (5) store weekly CSV exports for assessors.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=compliance sourcetype IN ("compliance:control_test","nessus:sc:compliance") earliest=-90d
| eval outcome=if(match(coalesce(test_result,status),"(?i)pass|passed|success"),1,0)
| bin _time span=7d
| stats count as tests, sum(outcome) as passes by _time
| eval org_pass_ratio=if(tests>0, round(100*passes/tests,2), null())
| sort _time
| trendline sma3(org_pass_ratio) as ratio_trend
| eventstats avg(org_pass_ratio) as portfolio_mean
| eval gap=round(org_pass_ratio-portfolio_mean,2)
| predict org_pass_ratio as ratio_forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=2 period=6
```

Understanding this SPL

**Control Effectiveness Trending** — A ninety-day pass ratio by control domain exposes domains where tests are failing more often or trending down—so you prioritize control owners, evidence collection, and automation before a failed external assessment.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=compliance` `sourcetype IN ("compliance:control_test","nessus:sc:compliance","qualys:policy")` — `control_domain`, `test_result` or `status`, `_time`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Tenable (Splunkbase 4060) for scan-based control checks, DB Connect for ITGC spreadsheets, or `compliance:control_test` automation feeds. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: compliance.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=compliance, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **outcome** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` aggregates events by **_time**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **org_pass_ratio** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

• `eventstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gap** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(Vulnerabilities.cvss) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest span=7d | sort - agg_value
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Control Effectiveness Trending** — A ninety-day pass ratio by control domain exposes domains where tests are failing more often or trending down—so you prioritize control owners, evidence collection, and automation before a failed external assessment.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=compliance` `sourcetype IN ("compliance:control_test","nessus:sc:compliance","qualys:policy")` — `control_domain`, `test_result` or `status`, `_time`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Tenable (Splunkbase 4060) for scan-based control checks, DB Connect for ITGC spreadsheets, or `compliance:control_test` automation feeds. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: _time, domain, eff_trend, gap, org_pass_ratio, outcome, pass_ratio, passes.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Multiseries line or area (pass_ratio by domain), heatmap (domain x week), line (`org_pass_ratio`, `ratio_trend`, `ratio_forecast`).
